title: Aspidium ridleyanum
intro: Aspidium ridleyanum, also known as Ridley's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the genus Aspidium. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium ridleyanum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aspidium ridleyanum is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Aspidium ridleyanum is a species of fern, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. This species falls under the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. The rank of order is Polypodiales, with the family Polypodiaceae being a part of it. Aspidium ridleyanum is further classified as a genus Aspidium which is now considered a synonym of the genus Drynaria. Therefore, the correct classification of Aspidium ridleyanum can be considered as Drynaria ridleyanum.
